questions.question: What can you earn by completing Notion Academy learning paths?

options.explanation: The article states that you can earn badges by completing curated Notion learning paths.
Certificates are only mentioned for the Notion Certified Admin program, not for general path completion.
The article makes no reference to college credits being awarded.
Badges are indeed awarded, so this option is incorrect.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT listed as a lesson title in the article?

options.explanation: Custom Agents is listed as a beginner lesson.
Sharing & collaboration appears in the lesson list.
Advanced SQL is not mentioned anywhere in the article.
Templates & marketplace is included among the intermediate lessons.

questions.question: How many lessons are included in the Essentials Path?

options.explanation: The article specifies a different number of lessons.
The Essentials Path is described as containing 3 lessons.
Four lessons belong to the Workflows Path, not the Essentials Path.
Five lessons belong to the Advanced Path.

questions.question: Which path is described as “Create connected systems that unite teams and projects, with the help of AI”?

options.explanation: Essentials Path focuses on basic skills, not AI‑driven workflows.
The article uses that exact phrasing for the Workflows Path.
Advanced Path emphasizes automation and architecture, not the specific AI‑connected systems description.
Project Management is a single lesson, not a multi‑lesson path.

questions.question: To become a Notion Certified Admin, which capability is expected of you?

options.text: Ability to run Notion at scale and implement security best practices
options.explanation: The certification description highlights running Notion at scale and applying security best practices.

options.text: Mastery of Python scripting for automation
options.explanation: Python is not mentioned as a requirement for the admin certification.
Marketing skills are unrelated to the admin certification focus.
Graphic design is not part of the admin certification criteria.

questions.question: Which lesson is categorized as intermediate and focuses on “An issue tracker for any team”?

options.explanation: Building basics is a beginner lesson about navigation and blocks.

options.text: An issue tracker for any team
options.explanation: The article lists this exact title as an intermediate lesson.
Templates & marketplace is an intermediate lesson but about creating and selling templates.
Custom Agents is a beginner lesson about automation.

questions.question: If a user wants to learn about “Workspace design”, which difficulty level and approximate duration should they expect?

options.explanation: The article lists “Workspace design” as an advanced lesson lasting 10 minutes.
20 min is the duration for the beginner “Building basics” lesson, not Workspace design.
15 min matches the “Sharing & collaboration” beginner lesson, not Workspace design.
40 min is the length of the intermediate “Templates & marketplace” lesson.

questions.question: Considering the available paths, which one includes the highest number of lessons, and what is its difficulty level?

options.explanation: The Advanced Path contains 5 lessons and is labeled as Advanced, the most lessons of any path listed.
Workflows Path has 4 lessons, fewer than the Advanced Path.
Essentials Path only includes 3 lessons.
Project Management is a single lesson, not a multi‑lesson path.
